text.title: Debt Types, Metrics, and Detection
text.summary: Fowler quadrant for debt classification, debt category taxonomy, complexity metrics, and automated detection patterns.

section.title: Fowler quadrant
section.p: Two axes: Reckless vs Prudent (intent) and Deliberate vs Inadvertent (awareness).

list.item: Reckless + Deliberate: "We don't have time for design" — high risk, avoid
Prudent + Deliberate: "We must ship now and deal with consequences" — strategic, document and track
Reckless + Inadvertent: "What's layering?" — incompetence, address via training and reviews
Prudent + Inadvertent: "Now we know how we should have done it" — learning, normal; refactor when the area is touched

rule.statement: Label every debt register entry with its quadrant. Deliberate+Prudent items need a paydown date; Inadvertent items get addressed opportunistically on next touch.
rule.rationale: The quadrant determines the urgency and mechanism: strategic debt needs a scheduled paydown; inadvertent debt can be fixed "on the way through".

list.item: code — duplication, poor naming, high cyclomatic complexity
design — tight coupling, wrong abstractions, missing domain boundaries
test — missing or flaky tests for critical paths
infra — outdated dependencies, missing CI/CD, manual deployments
docs — outdated or missing architecture/API documentation
process — missing code review, no branching strategy, manual release
dependency — pinned to EOL libraries, duplicate transitive versions

rule.statement: Use automated tools to evidence severity claims: cyclomatic complexity (radon/gocyclo/eslint), duplication % (jscpd), maintainability index (radon mi). Attach tool output to register entries. Never accept agent-invented complexity numbers.
rule.rationale: Subjective severity without tool output is gamed; evidence from static analysis makes scoring reproducible and audit-able.

list.item: Test coverage target: > 80% for business-critical paths
Linting violations target: 0 blocking (errors); warnings triaged weekly

section.title: Detection patterns

example.note: In-code TECH_DEBT marker with ID, description, severity, and interest. Scannable by the audit script.

section.reference: Surfaces unresolved TODO/HACK/FIXME with git blame metadata and age
